reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Real transformers.js text-classification in a dedicated module Worker (models/roberta-emotions/worker.js): loadPipeline({task:"text-classification", model:"SamLowe/roberta-base-go_emotions-onnx", backend:"wasm", dtype:"q8"}) then pipe(text,{top_k:28}) to return EVERY class score. Because the model config declares problem_type "multi_label_classification", transformers.js applies a per-class SIGMOID (not softmax) — the demo surfaces the real, unaltered head output. WASM demo (no WebGPU), runs everywhere. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean tested-pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. Latency/scores are NOT fabricated in this code-grounded backfill.
rubric.notes: A batch op scores many texts in one padded forward pass (for the sentence-arc + support-router rungs) — same shape per input.

rubric.evidence: Expected output is an array of {label,score} for all 28 GoEmotions classes (+ neutral), sorted high->low, each an INDEPENDENT 0–1 sigmoid that need NOT sum to 1 (several emotions can fire at once). The page renders a full ranked bar chart (emotions.js renderEmotionBars: one row per class, inline-size:${pct}% fill, rows above a threshold highlighted) — the honest multi-label view.
rubric.notes: The 'scores don't sum to 1' multi-label semantics is stated explicitly in at-a-glance and the copy.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (SamLowe/roberta-base-go_emotions-onnx, linked), task (text-classification, multi-label), the 28-class label set + per-class sigmoid, architecture (RoBERTa-base 125M fine-tuned on GoEmotions), quantisation (q8), backend (WebAssembly, no WebGPU), download (~125 MB), license MIT. Matches worker.js. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation + declares-backend tested-pass.

Loads via shared createModelLoader (modelId:"SamLowe/roberta-base-go_emotions-onnx", dtype:"q8", sizeMB:125, requiresWebGPU:false). Valid cached model auto-initialises with no Load click; Download/Re-download/Update + clear-cache otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source) tested-pass; auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on the repo so the ~125 MB cache is tracked for the no-silent-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence:
Status is

(runStatus); the bar-chart container is aria-live; controls (textarea, threshold slider) start disabled and enable onReady. On failure the worker posts {type:error} surfaced on the page. Conformance live-status-region + errors-on-the-page tested-pass; runs-without-webgpu-fallback asserted (needs no GPU); honest-progress-retry-offline manual.

Every control is real + keyboard-operable: the text textarea (default a gratitude/relief example), a threshold range slider (0.05–0.9, default 0.3) that re-highlights which classes count as 'fired', and the analyse action. The threshold genuinely changes the multi-label decision boundary shown.
No hardcoded scores: every bar is a real per-class sigmoid from pipe(text,{top_k:28}). Conformance no-fake-output manual-evidenced.

Status + results use role=status / aria-live; buttons have text names (buttons-have-names tested-pass); the page has no content images (images-have-alt vacuously tested-pass); the threshold slider is a labelled native input; light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: Minor: the emotion bars convey magnitude by width + a numeric % label; a role=meter/aria-valuenow on each bar (as toxic-bert does) would strengthen the non-visual reading.

rubric.evidence: Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are the tested assertions; the input + 28-row bar chart stack fluidly, bars are inline-size:%-based. parity-both-classes manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: WASM demo — fully usable on mobile; ~125 MB download surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Classification runs in the dedicated module Worker; the main thread only renders 28 bar rows (a bounded DOM update). Batch scoring is one padded forward pass off-thread. Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source: new Worker) tested-pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low run heavy work off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread and break up long tasks so the browser can service input and repaint; workers + yielding keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — classification (single + batch padded forward pass) runs in a dedicated module Worker; the page thread only renders the ranked bars.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker(type:module); conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates, accessible names for controls, loading state
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; semantic HTML + visible focus; mark language for correct shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live status, aria-live results, labelled threshold slider + textarea, accessible action names.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow using flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap, intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — input + 28-row bar chart stack fluidly with %-based bar widths; no horizontal overflow at mobile.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form controls: range slider and text input labelling, keyboard operable
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate every input with a label, keep controls keyboard-operable, and reflect state changes accessibly.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the threshold range slider and text input are labelled native controls, keyboard-operable, and re-render the decision boundary live.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: native <input type=range> + <textarea>; threshold re-highlights fired classes

openQuestions: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached are manual-evidenced — a headless WASM run could capture real 28-class scores + latency to flip them.
Should each emotion bar carry role=meter/aria-valuenow (like the toxic-bert bars) so the magnitudes are readable by assistive tech, not just visually?

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a real headless WASM classification run (28-class scores + latency) to satisfy io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached.
Add role=meter + aria-valuenow to the emotion bars to match the accessible-meter pattern used by toxicity-detection.

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique for roberta-emotions (SamLowe/roberta-base-go_emotions-onnx, text-classification MULTI-LABEL, q8, WASM — runs everywhere, ~125 MB, MIT). RoBERTa-base fine-tuned on GoEmotions: pipe(text,{top_k:28}) returns all 28 classes each as an INDEPENDENT per-class sigmoid (problem_type multi_label_classification — scores don't sum to 1), rendered as a full ranked bar chart with a live threshold, off the main thread. No WebGPU needed. No fabricated scores; latency NOT invented in this backfill. guidanceConsulted: break-up-long-tasks, accessibility, css-layout, forms. Weakness flagged: emotion bars lack role=meter/aria-valuenow (toxic-bert has it); io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
